label: Arduous / Difficult Work
description: The difficult good work whose completion and endurance are explicitly attached to fortitude and the gift of fortitude.

label: Beatitude of Hunger and Thirst for Justice
description: Beatitude pronounced on those hungering and thirsting for justice.

label: Charity
description: Theological virtue of friendship with God.
notes: Do not auto-normalize bare 'love' to charity outside clearly theological context.

label: Constancy
description: The fortitude-related habit of persisting in good against external hindrances rather than the mere duration of the act.
notes: Kept distinct from perseverance even though q.137 closely compares the two.

label: Continuance in Good
description: The continued accomplishment of a good work over time that is distinctive of perseverance.

label: Gift of Counsel
description: Gift of the Holy Spirit perfecting right counsel in action.

label: Effeminacy
description: The vice by which one forsakes a good on account of difficulties that cannot be endured, especially through softness regarding pleasure.

label: Fortitude
description: The cardinal virtue governing firmness in difficulties and dangers.

label: Gift of Fortitude
description: Gift of the Holy Spirit strengthening endurance in arduous good.

label: God
description: God as the object or term of theological virtue and charity.

label: Grace
description: Grace as the supernatural help needed beyond fallen nature.
notes: Generic grace remains distinct from operating and cooperating grace unless the text names the subtype.

label: Grievous Evil / Hardship
description: The difficult evil or sorrow endured by patience and, analogously, by fortitude.

label: Longanimity
description: The habit of tending toward a good that lies far off, compared with patience without being simply identical to it.
notes: Kept tract-local because q.136 explicitly resists collapsing longanimity into patience.

label: Magnanimity
description: The fortitude-related virtue by which a person deems himself worthy of great honors and great things in due proportion.
notes: Keep distinct from magnificence: magnanimity is honor- and worthiness-related rather than expenditure-related.

label: Magnificence
description: The fortitude-related virtue of producing great works with proportionate expenditure.
notes: Keep distinct from magnanimity: magnificence concerns great works and expenditure, not honor as such.

label: Patience
description: The annexed virtue by which one bears grievous evils with an equal mind for the sake of good.
notes: Keep distinct from fruit-level patience in later correspondence work.

label: Perseverance (Fortitude Part)
description: The virtue by which one persists long in a good work despite the difficulty arising from its continuance.
notes: This tract-local concept is kept distinct from the broader act-level `concept.perseverance` already present from the I-II grace pilot.

label: Pertinacity
description: The vice by which one persists more than one ought, exceeding the mean of perseverance.

label: Precepts of Fortitude
description: The precept-complex by which Divine Law directs fortitude itself toward adherence to God and brave endurance.

label: Precepts of Patience and Perseverance
description: The precept-complex by which Divine Law gives directions concerning patience and perseverance as annexed virtues.

label: Vainglory
description: Inordinate desire for empty glory, opposed in this tract to magnanimity's right use of honor and glory.

label: Virtue
description: Generic virtue as good operative perfection.
notes: Do not collapse generic virtue into a specific virtue without explicit passage support.
